channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NFL has no plans to end DEI practices, commissioner says
item.description: The NFL is committed to sticking to its DEI policies despite a change in its Super Bowl on-field messaging campaign
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says US Steel will get investment from Nippon Steel, instead of being bought by it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday suggested that Nippon Steel would no longer buy U.S. Steel as planned, but the Japanese company would instead invest in the symbolically important American business. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kash Patel had a roster of foreign clients. Their interests could clash with FBI he hopes to lead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, holds more than $1 million of stock in a fashion company founded in China. He established a nonprofit that spent big on promotion but little on its mission.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: More active duty troops will head to US-Mexico border, bringing the total to 3,600
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will deploy roughly 1,500 more active duty soldiers to the southern border to support President Donald Trump’s expanding crackdown on immigration, a U.S. official said Friday. That would eventually bring the total to about 3,600 active duty troops at the border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration orders federal agencies to provide lists of underperforming employees
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has ordered all federal departments and agencies to provide lists of employees who are underperforming, as it seeks to shrink the workforce and awaits a court ruling related to its deferred resignation offers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KREX 5

item.title: DOGE staffer who accessed Treasury Department payment systems resigns after racist posts exposed
item.description: “Normalize Indian hate,” the account associated with Marko Elez posted in September, regarding people of Indian ethnicity who work in the U.S. tech sector, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bering Air plane vanishes in Alaska while carrying 10 people
item.description: A desperate search is underway Friday in Alaska for a missing Bering Air plane that vanished Thursday while carrying 10 people on a flight from Unalakleet to Nome.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Reid G. Miller, longtime AP international correspondent and editor, dies at 90
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Reid G. Miller, who traveled the planet as an intrepid international correspondent for The Associated Press and developed a reputation as a supportive editor and unswervingly loyal boss during the toughest of breaking-news moments, has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: TED ANTHONY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Real estate agent commissions mostly unchanged since new NAR rules
item.description: New real estate commission rules took effect in August, but so far, not much has changed, according to a Redfin report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: 12 states to sue over DOGE access to payment systems containing personal data
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law," a statement from the Democratic attorneys general reads.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Daytona boss inspires students with his NASCAR journey
item.description: The "Super Bowl" of racing, the Daytona 500, will be the next biggest event in the world of sports, taking place February 16, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Davis Suppes, NewsNation

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Justice Sotomayor renews her opposition to the court's ruling that ex-presidents have broad immunity
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said her conservative colleagues are risking the court's legitmacy with decisions affording President Donald Trump broad immunity and overturning longstanding precedents on other issues.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Democrats delay Kash Patel committee vote
item.description: Voting on Patel's nomination was on the agenda for Judiciary members, but Democrats requested it be held over one week.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Cracker Barrel calls out Waffle House over egg surcharge
item.description: Cracker Barrel looks to be wooing Waffle House’s breakfast crowd with a new deal for loyal customers. The company announced Thursday that members of its Cracker Barrel Rewards program who order egg dishes through Wednesday, Feb.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho makes key move on firing squad executions
item.description: Idaho House Bill 37 aims to make the firing squad the state's primary means of execution as prosecutors prepare to try Bryan Kohberger in four college murders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New NASA imagery shows large asteroid that could impact Earth in 2032
item.description: A recently released animation shows a new look at a large asteroid that has a small, but significant, chance of impacting planet Earth in seven years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KRON4

item.title: Freezing hikers stuck in waist-deep snow rescued during Mount Washington whiteout
item.description: The two friends had hiked New Hampshire's challenging Mount Washington trail a month ago. They returned and did it again Sunday after trekking through neighboring peaks in the White Mountains.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A dozen West Point cadet clubs ordered to disband, all others paused
item.description: The U.S. Military Academy at West Point, New York, has ordered a dozen cadet extracurricular and social clubs to immediately and permanently "cease all activities," with all affected groups centered on gender, race and ethnicity, the school acknowledged Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Blake Lively is sued by Texas crisis specialist in latest 'It Ends With Us' lawsuit
item.description: A Texas crisis communications specialist has sued Blake Lively for defamation after the actor pulled him into her legal fight with co-star and director Justin Baldoni over their film, “It Ends With Us.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: CIA shares new employee names through unclassified email
item.description: The CIA used unclassified email to share a list of employees hired within the last two years to the Office of Personnel Management (OPM).
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KRON4

item.title: Taco Bell's Live Mas event moves from L.A. to New York City
item.description: Taco Bell’s Live Mas event, originally scheduled to take place at the Hollywood Palladium in Los Angeles, will move to New York City, company officials announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump signs executive order intended to bar transgender athletes from girls' and women's sports
item.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Wednesday intended to ban transgender athletes from participating in girls' and women’s sports.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 1 person dead and 5 wounded in shooting at Ohio cosmetics warehouse, police search for suspect
item.description: One person has died and five others were wounded in a shooting at a cosmetics warehouse in Ohio, prompting an ongoing search Wednesday for a male suspect.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims in deadly DC plane and chopper collision now recovered
item.description: The chief medical examiner is still trying to positively identify one set of remains, officials said in a news release.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries vows to use government shutdown fight to combat Trump orders
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Monday that Democrats will use the coming battle over government funding as a vehicle to block President Trump’s early efforts to gut federal programs — a warning that raises the chances of a shutdown in the middle of next month.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5
